note_data.title: Urban Settlements
keyPoints: Subsistence farming is growing crops for own family where as cash crop is growing for sale and profit purpose
In cash crop farms large fields are used with higher capital required for modern irrigation system i.e. perennical canals, tubewells, sprinklers
Cash crop farms use scientific methods e.g hyv seeds, machinery(tractors and harvestor) use of chemical fertilizers and greater use of pesticides and herbicides for plant protection
In small scall subsistence farming small land utlizes with low capital invested because of poverty
Subsistence farms use old irrigation methods like shaduf and desi variety of seeds are used
Most of the work in subsistence farming is done manually and done by the family with draft power utilized for ploughing
Barani farming depends entirely on rainfall/rain-fed area with low banks/bunds constructed and field size varies considerably
Barani farmers are too poor to own tractors/lack of machines/traditional methods and use animal dung/no fertiliser
Farming using natural rainfall/flooding can only be carried out after flooding/must wait for floods with farmers having no control of water supply

definitions.definition: Crops grown for sale/profit/commercial rather than for consumption by the farmers
Growing for sale and profit purpose
Farming that depends entirely on rainfall/rain-fed area

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Give characteristics and features. Use data from maps/diagrams if provided

modelAnswer.question: Explain why the CBD has the highest land values in a city. [3 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: The CBD has the highest land values because it is the most accessible location with excellent transport links from all directions. Land values are high because there is limited space but high demand from businesses wanting a central location. The CBD attracts the most customers because it is easily reached by public transport and has the greatest concentration of shops and services.
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Most accessible location/excellent transport links
Mark 2: Limited space but high demand from businesses
Mark 3: Attracts most customers/concentration of shops and services

commonMistakes: Confusing kharif and rabi seasons - wheat is rabi, rice is kharif
Not understanding that rice requires over 1275mm rainfall which nowhere in Pakistan has for barani farming
Mixing up subsistence and cash crop farming characteristics
Not recognizing that natural inundation farming must wait for floods while irrigation provides continuous water supply
examinerTips: For crop seasons, remember rabi requires mild/warm temperature 10degree-20degree for growing and 20degrees for ripening
When describing farming differences, contrast capital investment, field sizes, methods used and labor types
For natural conditions, include temperature ranges, rainfall amounts, soil types and seasonal requirements
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Concentric zone model showing CBD in centre, surrounded by rings of industrial zone, inner residential area, and outer suburbs, with land values and building heights decreasing outwards
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (6 marks) — they asked students to explain the factors that influence the location of urban settlements with reference to named examples
O/N 2022 P1 Q2 (4 marks) tested urban hierarchy by asking candidates to describe the characteristics that distinguish a city from a town
M/J 2022 P2 Q3b (8 marks) required students to assess the advantages and disadvantages of urban growth in developing countries
O/N 2021 P2 Q5 (10 marks) asked for an evaluation of strategies used to solve urban problems in cities, with specific case study examples

workedExample.question: Explain why urban settlements grow rapidly in developing countries. Use examples in your answer. (8 marks)
steps: Step 1: Define urban growth and identify it's faster in developing countries
Step 2: Explain push factors from rural areas (lack of jobs, poor services, natural disasters)
Step 3: Explain pull factors to cities (employment opportunities, better education/healthcare, perceived higher living standards)
Step 4: Add specific examples - Mumbai (Bollywood film industry jobs), Lagos (oil industry), São Paulo (manufacturing)
Step 5: Mention natural increase (young migrants having families in cities)
workedExample.answer: Urban settlements in developing countries grow rapidly due to rural-urban migration and natural increase. Push factors from rural areas include lack of employment opportunities, poor access to services like healthcare and education, and agricultural problems such as drought. Pull factors to cities include job opportunities in manufacturing and services, better infrastructure, and access to education. For example, Mumbai attracts migrants due to its film industry and financial services, while Lagos grows due to Nigeria's oil industry. São Paulo expanded rapidly due to manufacturing jobs. Additionally, young migrants often start families in cities, leading to natural population increase that sustains urban growth.
workedExample.markScheme: 2 marks for defining/identifying rapid urban growth, 2 marks for push factors with explanation, 2 marks for pull factors with explanation, 2 marks for relevant examples with specific details

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused urbanization with urban growth' — always define urbanization as the percentage increase of people living in towns/cities, not just population growth
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students frequently gave vague examples without specific details' — always name actual cities and explain specific industries or factors, not just 'better jobs'
Examiner Report 2021: 'Candidates often ignored the command word assess/evaluate' — when asked to evaluate urban solutions, always give both advantages AND disadvantages with a conclusion
note_data.keyDiagram: Urban Hierarchy Pyramid: Draw a triangle divided into 4 levels. Top: Megacities (over 10 million, few in number, widest range of services). Second: Cities (100,000-10 million, regional centers, specialized services like universities). Third: Towns (10,000-100,000, local services, market centers). Bottom: Villages (under 10,000, most numerous, basic services only). Add arrows showing population size decreasing upward and number of settlements decreasing upward. Label sphere of influence expanding at each level.
note_data.quickRecap: Pakistani agriculture includes both subsistence farming (for family consumption) and cash crop farming (for profit). Cash crop farms use larger fields, modern machinery, and scientific methods, while subsistence farms are smaller with traditional methods and family labor. Different farming systems like barani (rain-fed) and natural inundation depend on rainfall and flooding patterns. Crops are classified as kharif (summer) or rabi (winter) based on growing seasons and temperature requirements.
